summary_one_line: Input must carry the 4-byte ASCII FOURCC 'MCH3' (4d 43 48 33) at offset 0x10 as the profile colorspace signature.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace-signature field at offset 0x10 must equal the literal FOURCC 'MCH3' = 4d 43 48 33, which routes the switch in cmsChannelsOfColorSpace to the cmsSigMCH3Data case at line 897.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All cmplog winner seeds carry 4d(M) 43(C) 48(H) 33(3) at offsets 0x10-0x13 (12/12 in the W column), matching the cmsSigMCH3Data signature compared inside the switch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed instead carries 52(R) 47(G) 42(B) 20( ) = 'RGB ' at offsets 0x10-0x13, which routes to the cmsSigRgbData arm and never reaches the MCH3 case.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted CMP operands (the parser comparing the read FOURCC against the 'MCH3' constant), finds the seed's FOURCC bytes, and substitutes the constant operand at offset 0x10, planting 'MCH3' that naive cannot guess; the dash-row I2S-floor signal in the winner's lineage confirms an I2S find in its ancestry.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same 4-byte colorspace-signature field at offset 0x10 must equal 'MCH3' (4d 43 48 33) to take the cmsSigMCH3Data switch arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner seed carries 4d(M) 43(C) 48(H) 33(3) at offsets 0x10-0x13, the exact FOURCC the switch dispatcher tests against for cmsSigMCH3Data.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive losers hold 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at the same offsets, differing in all four FOURCC bytes, so the equality against the MCH3 signature never matches.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose FOURCC operand is progressively closer (matching-prefix length / Hamming bucket) to the 'MCH3' constant in the switch comparison, preserving partial-match seeds as corpus and letting havoc complete all four bytes; naive lacks this distance feedback and cannot climb to a full match.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the cmsSigMCH3Data switch case in cmsChannelsOfColorSpace at cmspcs.c:897.
Winner seeds carry 'MCH3' bytes at offsets 0x10-0x13 while losers carry 'RGB ' there.
Offset 0x11 differs: winners C, losers G.
Offset 0x12 differs: winners H, losers B.
A cmplog winner seed has 'MCH3' in its ASCII byte dump at offset 0x10.
A naive loser seed has 'RGB ' in its ASCII byte dump at offset 0x10.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a recorded CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP encodes how close the two CMP operands were, preserving closer-matching inputs as corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolved all 10 trials while naive blocked all 10.
cmplog resolved 8/10 trials while naive blocked all 10.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2297 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 3216f2b70a893768` and observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 2 and depth 3 of the ancestor chain; tool reported '2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 2, 3', direct lower-bound evidence of an I2S find in the cmplog winner's ancestry.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the same switch on a 4-byte FOURCC where cmplog and value_profile fail to resolve the 'MCH3'-equality arm faster than naive (no I2S/CMP_MAP advantage over havoc) would refute the comparison-solving attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The two pairs share one identical input feature (the MCH3 FOURCC), so attributing the value_profile and cmplog wins to distinct mechanisms rather than to the single 4-byte equality being trivially reachable by either technique is the most uncertain split.
